title: commands: rename file and add likns to readthedocs
body:
rename the commands.md to commands-demo.md per suggestion here: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4414

and update the links to point to the read the docs page

 - fixes https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4414


/cc @rhatdan

comments.body:
Hi @cpanato. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

The changes to commands-demo.md look awesome.  However, rather than removing command.md, I'd like to keep it but remove most of the contents and leave something like:

This page has moved [here](commands-demo.md)

thanks for the review @TomSweeneyRedHat 
made the change requested

@stevekuznetsov Sorry this keeps happening... Any chance we could get the robot to add a comment to the issue if it stalls for some length of time (~10 minutes, maybe) so folks like me who aren't actively monitoring CI load can see something is wrong?
@cpanato No problems on your end, CI is just flaking badly at the moment (we have an elusive bug in `podman exec` that only occurs on our CI setup)
It's hard for us to tell from the API why we're failing to merge something we think should be merge-able but I think surfacing it would be useful, yes

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cpanato*, *rhatdan*, *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
